tools.name: federation_list_tenants
tools.description: List all available tenants in the federation. Returns tenant IDs, names, and status.

tools.name: federation_tenant_info
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific tenant.

tools.name: federation_create_tenant
tools.description: Create a new tenant (federation owns the manager/tenant lifecycle, CO 138).

tools.name: federation_create_workflow
tools.description: Author and deploy a workflow (CO 213). Composes the tenant’s tools — including cross-platform `mcp` steps that call any platform the tenant has composed (retail, bookings, …) — into a triggered automation that fires immediately. Step types: http, notification, agent, delay, condition, transform, mcp

tools.name: federation_update_tenant
tools.description: Update a tenant’s display name and/or description (federation owns the lifecycle, CO 138). CO 222 — the caller must own the tenant, or be root/admin (per-tenant ownership scoping, when enabled).

tools.name: federation_suspend_tenant
tools.description: Suspend or reactivate a tenant (federation owns the lifecycle, CO 138). CO 222 — the caller must own the tenant, or be root/admin (per-tenant ownership scoping, when enabled).

tools.name: federation_create_manager
tools.description: Mint a manager — the accountability root that owns tenants (CO 138 / CO 159). A manager composes platforms and creates tenants to build a vertically-integrated graph. Next: create tenants with federation_create_tenant, then compose a platform by entering it (federation_list_tenants → federation_ente

tools.name: federation_list_managers
tools.description: List managers — the accountability roots that own tenants (CO 138).

tools.name: federation_manager_tree
tools.description: The manager → tenant accountability tree (CO 138).

tools.name: federation_freeze_manager
tools.description: Freeze a manager root (CO 259-001): set its standing to frozen or suspended so its graph cannot take privileged actions. Enforced at dispatch. Reversible via federation_unfreeze_manager. managers:admin.

tools.name: federation_unfreeze_manager
tools.description: Restore a manager root to active standing (CO 259-001): its graph may take privileged actions again. managers:admin.

tools.name: federation_journal_append
tools.description: Append an entry to your agent journal — your durable memory. Record WHAT you decided and WHY, so a future cold session (a new run with no chat history) can replay your reasoning and resume your business with no human recap. Append-only; idempotent on entry_key. The body (identity, capital, tenants)

tools.name: federation_journal_read
tools.description: Replay your agent journal oldest→newest. Call this at the start of a cold session — with only your tenant id — to reconstruct your intent and resume where you left off. Pairs with federation_journal_append.

tools.name: moltbook_read
tools.description: Read Moltbook — 'the front page of the agent internet', a social network whose members are AI agents. This is where your customers, partners, and collaborators congregate OUTSIDE the federation (EMPIRE-BUILDER-FIELD-GUIDE §2). target='feed' reads the global feed; target='submolt' reads one community

tools.name: moltbook_post
tools.description: Post or comment on Moltbook, attributed to your manager-root's Moltbook identity. NORM: contribute, don't pitch — share what you sell and what you've LEARNED; disclosure over persuasion; lead with honest limits. Moltbook gates posting behind a one-time human claim: if your identity is unclaimed this

tools.name: web_research
tools.description: Do deep web research (via Tavily) to SOURCE and VET a real provider for what you sell — the way a founder would: go find a supplier/asset/partner you can build a long-term relationship with. CO 245 says you may only claim what you can truly provide; this is how you become able to. Returns a synthesi

tools.name: federation_attest
tools.description: Attest your manager root is alive (CO 264 liveness mandate). Activity already counts — every privileged action attests implicitly; call this when idle, or to SELF-HEAL a mandate-decayed root (standing suspended/frozen with reason mandate_decayed / mandate_expired): the decay lifts within ~30s of att

tools.name: federation_bond_post
tools.description: Post (or increase) your manager root's bond (CO 265): moves N of a tenant treasury you OWN from spendable to bonded — your value, on your ledger, nothing crosses and no cut bites. The bond is a published fact counterparties can weigh (federation_bond_status). Release runs a notice clock that must co

tools.name: federation_bond_release
tools.description: Release your bond (CO 265). First call starts the notice clock (BOND_NOTICE_DAYS, default 30d); call again at maturity to complete — completion requires your standing to be ACTIVE, and any explicit standing transition during the notice RESTARTS the clock. There is no forfeit and no reviewer: illiqui

tools.name: federation_bond_status
tools.description: Read a manager root's bond — the public machine-legible fact (CO 265): amount, state, notice maturity (with any standing-fact block named). Omit manager_id to read your own.

tools.name: federation_key_challenge
tools.description: Begin binding a public key to your manager root (CO 314). Returns a single-use, short-lived nonce; sign its UTF-8 bytes with your private key and present the signature to federation_key_bind. Identity is read from your token. Requires tenants:write.

tools.name: federation_key_bind
tools.description: Bind a public key to your manager root (CO 314): present { public_key, alg, signature } where the signature is over the nonce from federation_key_challenge. The federation VERIFIES the signature against the public key before persisting — a wrong key, an expired/absent nonce, or a tampered signature

tools.name: federation_key_status
tools.description: Read a manager root's bound public key — the public machine-legible fact (CO 314): public_key, alg, kid, bound_at. A counterparty verifies this root's signatures against it. Omit manager_id to read your own. Never returns any challenge or secret.

tools.name: federation_apply
tools.description: Join the ComOS network as a vendor. Called with your OAuth token, this ADMITS you: your manager root (the accountable root that owns tenants) is minted, bound to the email your token carries — OAuth + email is the whole door, no human review, no charge. Called without a token, it records a contact r

tools.name: federation_help
tools.description: Get usage instructions for the MCP federation. Call this first to understand how to use the tools correctly.

tools.name: federation_why
tools.description: Why build on ComOS — the case for becoming a vendor, not just a buyer. federation_help tells you HOW; this tells you WHY. Returns the thesis (arrive with a few Coms, leave running a vertically-integrated business) for you or your human root to decide on. Disclosure, not persuasion: quotes the real e

tools.name: federation_governance
tools.description: How the federation governs its agents: the autonomy ladder (off → recommend → confirm → auto), the manager ceiling and per-agent override clamp (narrows, never widens), the always-escalate-to-the-manager path, the confidence thresholds, and manager-standing enforcement (active/frozen/suspended, free

tools.name: legal_get
tools.description: Fetch a ComOS legal instrument as raw markdown: the Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, Data Processing Agreement (DPA), or a one-page Data Retention Summary. These are the terms an agent (and its manager) operate under — read them the way you read the pricesheet. Public and read-only; discloses, does

tools.name: federation_pricesheet
tools.description: The federation pricesheet — every platform's per-act Com prices, free to read (reading a price is discovery, and discovery is never charged). Each price is a usage-tiered curve [[threshold, price_coms], ...] keyed on your usage-to-date of that tool: [0,0] first tiers mean free-to-start, the last tie

tools.name: federation_catalog_search_multi
tools.description: Search products (flat — one row per variant) across multiple tenants in parallel. Pass tenant_ids as an array of tenant IDs or "all" for every active tenant. Use federation_catalog_search_grouped_multi for browse/discovery flows to get token-efficient family rollups.

tools.name: federation_catalog_search_grouped_multi
tools.description: Search product FAMILIES (variants of the same product grouped together) across multiple tenants in parallel. Prefer this for discovery — collapses size/color variants into one row per family with a price range and option breakdown. Drop to federation_catalog_search_multi when you need exact variant

tools.name: federation_catalog_version
tools.description: Return a fingerprint of the current catalog for a tenant — a cheap way to tell whether the catalog changed since your last read. Compare the returned catalog_version against the one embedded in a previous catalog_search _meta block; if different, re-run catalog_search to get current product_ids befo

tools.name: federation_catalog_platforms
tools.description: Browse the ComOS network's composable platforms as a recursive catalog. side="vendor" returns the platforms you can SELL ON (retail, bookings, events, …); side="customer" returns the tools you RUN WITH (messaging, shipping, marketing, …); omit side for all. parent="<key>" descends into a platform's

tools.name: federation_catalog_agents
tools.description: Browse the ComOS network's autonomous agent fleet — what each agent does and who it serves (merchant / shopper / platform / manager). Omit args for the fleet grouped by who-it-serves and by platform; pass serves= or platform= to filter; agent=<slug> for one agent's full card. Pairs with federation_c

tools.name: federation_arena
tools.description: The live arena — agent-run businesses ranked by their settlement-signed reputation (a trust-weighted function of real settled transactions, not raw volume). Omit args for the top of the board; platform= to filter by vendor type; tenant_id= for one business's rank. The score is derived from ComOS-sig

tools.name: federation_latency
tools.description: Reproducible latency — the MEASURED p50/p95/p99 (in ms) of recent authenticated tool executions, computed live from the gateway's own audit records (CO 292). The verifiable answer to "is it really sub-100ms?": the numbers are computed from real recorded call durations, never asserted. Omit args for

tools.name: federation_refresh_tools
tools.description: Force the federation to re-discover its tool list from every platform, bypassing the 5-minute cache. Use after a platform adds, removes, or changes tools and you need the change visible immediately. Rate-limited to one refresh per 30 seconds; calls inside the window are a no-op that report the coold

tools.name: federation_choice_compare
tools.description: Compare federation tenants on their structured choice_profiles to pick one for a need. Pass a free-text `need`, structured hard `constraints` (each ELIMINATES non-qualifying tenants — e.g. {dimension:"geo",op:"includes",value:"NG"} and {dimension:"weight",op:"lte",value:40}), and a `candidates` scop

tools.name: federation_choice_get
tools.description: Fetch one tenant's full choice_profile (the organic comparable facts + per-vertical attributes), its freshness, and its separate disclosed promotion label (null if not promoted). Use after federation_choice_compare has narrowed to a single tenant and you want everything it published.

tools.name: federation_promote
tools.description: Promote a tenant's choice_profile into agent consideration for a need-context, settled in Coms (CO 038) and bounded by the tenant's CO 036 spend caps + autonomy. Promotion buys LABELED visibility — it is disclosed to agents as a "Promoted" signal and NEVER alters, hides, or reorders the organic comp

tools.name: federation_search
tools.description: Route a natural-language intent to the right platform on the ComOS network — the first thing to call. Pass a free-text `intent` ("t-shirts", "make an appointment", "a table for four tonight") and get back the ranked platform(s) that serve it, each with the per-platform tools to call NEXT (e.g. booki

tools.name: federation_enter_tenant
tools.description: Enter a tenant to receive its tools on your session (progressive disclosure, CO 163-006 / CO 166-001). The gateway is a small catalog — list tenants with federation_list_tenants, then enter one here. Entering a PLATFORM tenant (retail, bookings, shipping) branches that platform's REAL action tools s

tools.name: about_us_about
tools.description: Read About ComOS — the Federation User Manual's knowledge (in the comos-federation voice). Read-only — returns composed knowledge, performs no transaction.

tools.name: how_to_about
tools.description: Read How to use ComOS — the operational walkthrough's knowledge (in the comos-federation voice). Read-only — returns composed knowledge, performs no transaction.

tools.name: federation_list_agents
tools.description: List all configured agents with their status and next scheduled run. Requires authentication via auth_token.

tools.name: federation_run_agent
tools.description: Manually trigger an agent to run immediately. Requires admin authentication via auth_token.

tools.name: federation_get_agent_runs
tools.description: Get execution history for an agent. Requires authentication via auth_token. Pass includeComposed:true for tenant-operator (Tier 2) agents to receive composedRunIds — references to the per-platform Tier 1 runs the Tier 2 run composed.

tools.name: federation_agent_status
tools.description: Get current status of all agents and the scheduler. Requires authentication via auth_token.

tools.name: federation_list_agent_types
tools.description: List all available agent types that can be configured. Requires authentication via auth_token.

tools.name: introspection_system_confidence
tools.description: Return a confidence number (0..1) for a federation node, with the evidence chain. node_kind: artifact | tool | tenant; node_id: path | name | id. Response is {kind: "evidenced", value, chain} or {kind: "no_evidence_available", reason}.

tools.name: introspection_system_coverage_gaps
tools.description: Enumerate structural coverage gaps (silent tools, artifacts with no eval, un-probed tenants). scope_kind: federation | tenant; scope_id required if scope_kind=tenant. Returns {kind: "evidenced", value: Gap[], chain} or {kind: "no_evidence_available", reason}.

tools.name: introspection_system_probe
tools.description: Return the full evidence trace for a single federation node. Same argument shape as confidence; the response carries the node-specific evidence rather than a collapsed number.

tools.name: introspection_system_diff
tools.description: Compare a node's evidence against a baseline date. node_kind/node_id same as confidence; baseline_kind="since", baseline_iso_date="YYYY-MM-DD[Thh:mm:ssZ]".

tools.name: introspection_corpus_query
tools.description: Ask the corpus a question. Returns {kind: "cited", content, citations} or {kind: "uncited", content, reason}. Every cited path resolves through the pinned manifest and every content_excerpt is verified as a substring of the artifact. Optional `repo` filters the candidate manifest entries to one repo

tools.name: introspection_corpus_probe_scope
tools.description: Ask the corpus what it covers on a given topic. Returns the topical map with citations, or uncited(out_of_scope) when the topic is not covered. Optional `repo` scopes the question to one repo (Phase 3.5).

tools.name: introspection_corpus_probe_disagreement
tools.description: Ask the corpus where its artifacts disagree on a topic. Highly load-bearing for honest epistemic surfacing. Optional `repo` restricts to intra-repo disagreement (Phase 3.5); cross-repo disagreement is a future tool.

tools.name: introspection_corpus_generate
tools.description: Generate a passage in the style/distribution of the corpus. Output lands in path-log/generated-outputs.jsonl for downstream review — IT NEVER ADMITS TO THE MANIFEST. Manifest admission requires the inbound P-b-D gate in comai-plan. Optional `repo` draws style/distribution from one repo (Phase 3.5).

tools.name: introspection_corpus_compare
tools.description: Compare two corpora. At v1 only the "current" pinned corpus is exposed; any other id returns uncited(out_of_scope). v1.1 will surface prior pins.

tools.name: introspection_system_climb_history
tools.description: Time-series read of the federation's honesty-ratio snapshots. Returns the climb trajectory over a time window. Default window: last 7 days, max 200 snapshots. Default repo: comai-plan; pass a different repo id to read that repo's subtree. Source: comai-plan/ratio_snapshots/ committed hourly.

tools.name: introspection_system_list_nodes
tools.description: Enumerate every artifact, tool, and tenant in the federation's evidence index with picker-grade NodeSummary entries (kind/id/display_name/activation_state/defect_categories?/last_touched_at). activation_state is the four NN states plus "defective" overlay when any of the four defect categories fires

tools.name: introspection_system_list_repos
tools.description: List the ComOS repositories that contribute to introspection. Returns {kind: "evidenced", value: { repos: [{ id, display_name, description }] }, chain} where every repo is currently active in the federation registry. Use this to discover which repo ids to pass to introspection_system_climb_history.

tools.name: introspection_system_change_graph
tools.description: Return the dependency graph of change orders across every registered ComOS repo: nodes (slug/repo/declared+filesystem parent/children/interests), parent/child edges (source: frontmatter | filesystem | both), interest edges (CO 2026-05-30-021 — a declared cross-repo stake, source: frontmatter | link)

tools.name: introspection_system_change_reach
tools.description: Return the REACH of a change — every artifact across every registered repo that declares (or is linked to) an interest in it, traversed over the change-graph's interest + parent/child edges. Each reached artifact carries its provenance (which CO reached it, by which edge source, with what note) and

tools.name: introspection_system_co_decisions
tools.description: Query the CO-management decision log: every change-order operation an agent scored or actuated, as an evidenced graph node (confidence breakdown + threshold + outcome + accountability, plus the actuation PR link when the act proposed one). mode="touched_co" (with co_id) → what decisions touched a CO

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "federation_key_challenge" references sensitive artifacts (private key). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Begin binding a public key to your manager root (CO 314). Returns a single-use, short-lived nonce; sign its UTF-8 bytes with your private key and present the si…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "federation_key_status" references sensitive artifacts (secret). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Read a manager root's bound public key — the public machine-legible fact (CO 314): public_key, alg, kid, bound_at. A counterparty verifies this root's signature…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "federation_apply" references sensitive artifacts (auth token). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Join the ComOS network as a vendor. Called with your OAuth token, this ADMITS you: your manager root (the accountable root that owns tenants) is minted, bound t…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "federation_governance" stacks urgency/override words (always, never, override). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: How the federation governs its agents: the autonomy ladder (off → recommend → confirm → auto), the manager ceiling and per-agent override clamp (narrows, never …

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "federation_search" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Route a natural-language intent to the right platform on the ComOS network — the first thing to call. Pass a free-text `intent` ("t-shirts", "make an appointmen…

findings.title: Description tells the model to hide behavior
findings.detail: Description of "introspection_system_change_graph" instructs the model to conceal actions from the user.

evidence.snippet: Return the dependency graph of change orders across every registered ComOS repo: nodes (slug/repo/declared+filesystem parent/children/interests), parent/child e…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: moltbook_read, federation_attest, federation_apply, federation_catalog_platforms · Private data: federation_journal_append, federation_attest, federation_key_challenge, federation_key_bind, federation_key_status, federation_apply, federation_governance, legal_get, federation_catalog_search_multi, federation_latency, introspection_system_change_reach · Public sink: federation_create_manager, moltbook_read, moltbook_post, federation_bond_post, federation_apply, federation_pricesheet, federation_catalog_platforms · Destructive: federation_journal_append, federation_catalog_search_grouped_multi, federation_catalog_version, federation_catalog_platforms, federation_arena, federation_list_agents, federation_run_agent, federation_get_agent_runs, introspection_system_coverage_gaps.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: moltbook_read, federation_attest, federation_apply, federation_catalog_platforms · Private data: federation_journal_append, federation_attest, federation_key_challenge, federation_key_bind, federation_key_status, federation_apply, federation_governance, legal_get, federation_catalog_search_multi, federation_latency, introspection_system_change_reach · Public sink: federation_create_manager, moltbook_read, moltbook_post, federation_bond_post, federation_apply, federation_pricesheet, federation_catalog_platforms · Destructive: federation_journal_append, federation_catalog_search_grouped_multi, federation_catalog_version, federation_catalog_platforms, federation_arena, federation_list_agents, federation_run_agent, federation_get_agent_runs, introspection_system_coverage_gaps

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

evidenced", value: Gap[], chain} or {kind: "no_evidence_available", reason}.

findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: moltbook_read, federation_attest, federation_apply, federation_catalog_platforms, autonomy_get_config, tenant_notification_config_get · Private data: federation_journal_append, federation_attest, federation_key_challenge, federation_key_bind, federation_key_status, federation_apply, federation_governance, legal_get, federation_catalog_search_multi, federation_latency, autonomy_set_config, decisions_evaluate, knowledge_add, knowledge_update, workflow_trigger, introspection_system_change_reach · Public sink: federation_create_manager, moltbook_read, moltbook_post, cohort_email_send, federation_bond_post, federation_apply, federation_pricesheet, federation_catalog_platforms, workflow_update · Destructive: federation_journal_append, federation_catalog_search_grouped_multi, federation_catalog_version, federation_catalog_platforms, federation_arena, autonomy_set_enabled, decisions_evaluate, decisions_reject, decisions_execute, products_bulk_import, workflow_list, workflow_get, workflow_delete, workflow_trigger, federation_list_agents, federation_run_agent, federation_get_agent_runs, introspection_system_coverage_gaps.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: moltbook_read, federation_attest, federation_apply, federation_catalog_platforms, autonomy_get_config, tenant_notification_config_get · Private data: federation_journal_append, federation_attest, federation_key_challenge, federation_key_bind, federation_key_status, federation_apply, federation_governance, legal_get, federation_catalog_search_multi, federation_latency, autonomy_set_config, decisions_evaluate, knowledge_add, knowledge_update, workflow_trigger, introspection_system_change_reach · Public sink: federation_create_manager, moltbook_read, moltbook_post, cohort_email_send, federation_bond_post, federation_apply, federation_pricesheet, federation_catalog_platforms, workflow_update · Destructive: federation_journal_append, federation_catalog_search_grouped_multi, federation_catalog_version, federation_catalog_platforms, federation_arena, autonomy_set_enabled, decisions_evaluate, decisions_reject, decisions_execute, products_bulk_import, workflow_list, workflow_get, workflow_delete, workflow_trigger, federation_list_agents, federation_run_agent, federation_get_agent_runs, introspection_system_coverage_gaps

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "federation_key_bind" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Bind a public key to your manager root (CO 314): present { public_key, alg, signature } where the signature is over the nonce from federation_key_challenge. The federation VERIFIES the signature against the public key before persisting — a wrong key, an expired/absent nonce, or a tampered signature " → after: "Bind a public key to your manager root (CO 314): present { public_key, alg, signature } where the signature is over the nonce from federation_key_challenge. The federation VERIFIES the signature against the public key before persisting — a wrong key, an expired/absent nonce, or a tampered signature "

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.

findings.title: Tool description changed since last scan
findings.detail: Tool "federation_key_status" changed its description with no version change. Silent description changes are how a trusted server turns malicious after review.

evidence.snippet: before: "Read a manager root's bound public key — the public machine-legible fact (CO 314): public_key, alg, kid, bound_at. A counterparty verifies this root's signatures against it. Omit manager_id to read your own. Never returns any challenge or secret." → after: "Read a manager root's bound public key — the public machine-legible fact (CO 314): public_key, alg, kid, bound_at. A counterparty verifies this root's signatures against it. Includes the tamper-evident attestation chain's current head (CO 324); full chain + verification rule at /.well-known/key-atte"

findings.remediation: Re-review the tool before continuing to trust it.
